macro_segments.title: Introduction and Review of Previous Discussion on Shinui Koneh and Agency

micro_segments.title: Opening and September 11 Memorial
Review of Shinui Koneh Debate: Rabbi Yehuda vs. Rabbi Meir
Application to Money Investment and Shlichus (Agency)
Heter Iska: The Workaround for Interest Prohibition

macro_segments.title: Purchase of a Field in Someone Else's Name and Conditions of Sale

micro_segments.title: Tanu Rabbanan: Buying a Field in a Friend's Name Without Condition
Rav Sheshet's Interpretation: Protecting Investment from Nuisance Suits
Challenge from Bnei Maarava: Must You Inform the Seller?
Abaye's Reading: Separating the Real Document from the Protective One
Forcing the Seller to Write a Second Document When Condition is Stipulated
Informing Witnesses vs. the Seller of the Condition

macro_segments.title: Case of Rav Kahana: Buying Flax Futures and the Problem of Ribbit

micro_segments.title: Setup: Money Given for Flax, Price Rises, Question of Collecting Profit
Must the Agent Sell in the Principal's Name to Avoid Ribbit Appearance
Gemara's Response: Flax Was Own in Interim, Either Way Profit is Legitimate
Rav's Principle: Amana in Produce, Not in Money; No Meshicha Problem
How Selling in Agent's Name Makes It Permissible Despite No Possession

macro_segments.title: The Stolen Object Obligation: Introduction to Gezel and Shevuat Sheker

micro_segments.title: Transition from Shinui Koneh to the Gezel Parsha and Oath Obligation
Mishna: Conditions of Returning a Stolen Object (Gezel)
Must Travel to Maddai: The Extent of the Obligation When False Oath Taken
Cannot Give to Son or Messenger; Must Give to Beit Din Messenger
If Victim Dies, Return to Heirs; Heirs' Status in Kapparah
Principal and Fifth: What Must Be Returned and Forgiveness Scenarios
Fifth on Fifth: Repeated False Oaths and Compounding Penalties
Various Cases: Gezel, Pikadon, Loan, Lost Object—All Subject to Same Oath Consequence

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Analysis of Whose View the Mishna Represents

micro_segments.title: Question: Is Traveling to Maddai Required Only After False Oath?
Gemara: Mishna Does Not Reflect Rabbi Tarfon or Rabbi Akiva's View Unqualified
Rabbi Tarfon vs. Rabbi Akiva: Stolen from One of Five Unknown People
Interpreting the Debate: Limited to Case of False Oath (Shevuat Sheker)
Rabbi Elazar ben Rabbi Tzadok: Takana When Expense Exceeds Principal
Rabbi Akiva's Rejection of Takana When Victim Uncertain to Receive Money
Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar: Debate Only Applies to Gezel, Not Honest Purchase
Challenge from Rava: Story of Chasid Paying Both Sellers (No Oath Mentioned)
Can a Chasid Ever Have Sworn Falsely? Gemara's Objection
Conclusion: Debate Exists Without False Oath; Tomorrow Will Match to Mishna

topical_tags.term: Heter Iska (business arrangement workaround for interest)
Bnei Maarava (Western Rabbis' view on agent naming)
Reish Galuta (Exilarch, used as protective name)
Chomesh (fifth penalty for stolen object and false oath)
Beit Din messenger (court's agent for returning stolen goods)
Rabbi Tarfon (Tanna: lenient on gezel without oath)
Rabbi Akiva (Tanna: stringent on gezel, must ensure return)
Rabbi Elazar ben Rabbi Tzadok (Tanna: takana for high expense cases)
Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar (Tanna: distinguishes gezel from honest purchase)
Pikadon (item held for safekeeping, subject to oath consequence)
Aveidah (lost object, subject to oath consequence)
